html:
The Thulecite Crown is a craftable Item found in the Ancient Tab. It requires 4 Nightmare Fuel, 4 Thulecite and an Ancient Pseudoscience Station to craft. It also has a 33% chance to be found inside a Large Ornate Chest. Like all items from the Ancient Tab, Thulecite Crowns cannot be prototyped and can only be crafted when near an Ancient Pseudoscience Station.

The Thulecite Crown is head Armor that will absorb 90% of physical damage and has 1200 durability. When the wearer takes damage from an attack, it has a 33% chance to create a force field. The force field lasts for 4 seconds, preventing all damage from attacks (except for Fire) and granting stun immunity. While the force field is active, 5% of incoming damage is reduced from Sanity. The force field cannot occur more than once every 9 seconds. Unequipping the Crown will disable an active force field, even though the animation remains. The Crown's force field emits enough light to protect the player from Charlie.


Downloadable Content

In the Reign of Giants DLC, players can gain more thulecite with the use of Construction Amulets and Deconstruction Staffs.

In the Shipwrecked DLC, Thulecite Crowns can be obtained as a possible reward from the Slot Machine and X Marks the Spot treasure Chests. It also can always be found in a Chest of the Depths left behind by the Quacken after its death, making Thulecite Crowns renewable also in Shipwrecked.

Tips

  • As it is clear from its perk, the Thulecite Crown is more suited for tanking enemies instead of kiting them.
  • The Crown is really useful when facing big groups of Mobs like Spiders, Hounds, and Birchnutters, as the force field will protect the players from being stunlocked.
  • The force field will protect from the special effects of enemy attacks, such as those of Frogs.
  • The Crown is a great armor for Willow, Maxwell, and Wigfrid ( only) since these characters can regain their sanity easily.
    • On the other hand, the Crown is not efficient when used on Woodlegs , since his maximum sanity value is lower than other characters and drops constantly when on land.
  • It is generally advised to also wear disposable body armor to increase the life of the Thulecite Crown. In Don't Starve Together, the Log Suit can absorb roughly 47% of already absorbed damage, effectively increasing the life of the Thulecite Crown from 840 to 1585.
  • In the Hamlet DLC, since the Thulecite Crown only triggers its force field when the wearer takes damage, the Vortex Cloak will prevent it from activating when both are worn simultaneously and the Thulecite Crown is the primary armor.[Verification Needed] If the Vortex Cloak is the primary armor, the Thulecite Crown will not lose durability but also will not trigger the force field. If the Vortex Cloak is at 0% durability, the Thulecite Crown will activate its force field. As such, using both pieces of equipment in combat at the same time is ill-advised in favor of something more disposable such as a Football Helmet.

Trivia

  • The Thulecite Crown was added in A Moderately Friendly Update.
  • Although the crown showcases a Red Gem, one does not need it to craft the crown.

Gallery

  • Thulecite Crown Equipped
  • The Thulecite Crown's force field.
  • Wilson wearing a Thulecite Crown and a Thulecite Suit, while holding a Thulecite Club.
  • Wilson wearing a Thulecite Crown.

  • Winona wearing a Thulecite Crown.
  • Wortox wearing a Thulecite Crown.
  • Wurt wearing a Thulecite Crown.

“It's no toque, but it'll do.”
–Woodie
“Fuel must flow.”

“A solid field is being created out of the dark fuel. What a revelation!”
–Wagstaff
“A cröwn, that fights!”
–Wigfrid
“And now we are king.”

–Wortox
“Queen Wurt!!”
–Wurt
“I'm the king! You have to listen to what I say!”
–Walter
“I'm never taking it off!”
–Wanda
The Thulecite Crown is a craftable Item found in the Ancient Tab. It requires 4 Nightmare Fuel, 4 Thulecite and an Ancient Pseudoscience Station to craft. It also has a 33% chance to be found inside a Large Ornate Chest. Like all items from the Ancient Tab, Thulecite Crowns cannot be prototyped and can only be crafted when near an Ancient Pseudoscience Station.
The Thulecite Crown is head Armor that will absorb 90% of physical damage and has 1200 durability. When the wearer takes damage from an attack, it has a 33% chance to create a force field. The force field lasts for 4 seconds, preventing all damage from attacks (except for Fire) and granting stun immunity. While the force field is active, 5% of incoming damage is reduced from Sanity. The force field cannot occur more than once every 9 seconds. Unequipping the Crown will disable an active force field, even though the animation remains. The Crown's force field emits enough light to protect the player from Charlie.
Downloadable Content[edit | edit source]
In the Reign of Giants DLC, players can gain more thulecite with the use of Construction Amulets and Deconstruction Staffs.
In the Shipwrecked DLC, Thulecite Crowns can be obtained as a possible reward from the Slot Machine and X Marks the Spot treasure Chests. It also can always be found in a Chest of the Depths left behind by the Quacken after its death, making Thulecite Crowns renewable also in Shipwrecked.
Tips[edit | edit source]
As it is clear from its perk, the Thulecite Crown is more suited for tanking enemies instead of kiting them.
The Crown is really useful when facing big groups of Mobs like Spiders, Hounds, and Birchnutters, as the force field will protect the players from being stunlocked.
The force field will protect from the special effects of enemy attacks, such as those of Frogs.
The Crown is a great armor for Willow, Maxwell, and Wigfrid ( only) since these characters can regain their sanity easily.
On the other hand, the Crown is not efficient when used on Woodlegs , since his maximum sanity value is lower than other characters and drops constantly when on land.
It is generally advised to also wear disposable body armor to increase the life of the Thulecite Crown. In Don't Starve Together, the Log Suit can absorb roughly 47% of already absorbed damage, effectively increasing the life of the Thulecite Crown from 840 to 1585.
In the Hamlet DLC, since the Thulecite Crown only triggers its force field when the wearer takes damage, the Vortex Cloak will prevent it from activating when both are worn simultaneously and the Thulecite Crown is the primary armor.[Verification Needed] If the Vortex Cloak is the primary armor, the Thulecite Crown will not lose durability but also will not trigger the force field. If the Vortex Cloak is at 0% durability, the Thulecite Crown will activate its force field. As such, using both pieces of equipment in combat at the same time is ill-advised in favor of something more disposable such as a Football Helmet.
Trivia[edit | edit source]
The Thulecite Crown was added in A Moderately Friendly Update.
Although the crown showcases a Red Gem, one does not need it to craft the crown.

summaryViHtml:
Tóm tắt

Vương Miện Thulecite là một Vật Phẩm có thể chế tạo được tìm thấy trong Thẻ Cổ. Nó cần 4 Nhiên Liệu Ác Mộng, 4 Thulecite và Trạm Giả Khoa Học Cổ Đại để chế tạo. Nó cũng có 33% cơ hội được tìm thấy bên trong Rương trang trí công phu lớn. Giống như tất cả các vật phẩm từ Thẻ Cổ, Vương Miện Thulecite không thể tạo nguyên mẫu và chỉ có thể chế tạo khi ở gần Trạm Giả Khoa Cổ Đại.

You have to listen to what I say! |wanda = I'm never taking it off! |choose = wendy}} The '''Thulecite Crown''' is a craftable [[Items|Item]] found in the [[Ancient Tab]]. It requires 4 [[Nightmare Fuel]], 4 [[Thulecite]] and an [[Ancient Pseudoscience Station]] to craft. It also has a 33% chance to be found inside a [[Large Ornate Chest]]. Like all items from the Ancient Tab, Thulecite Crowns cannot be prototyped and can only be crafted when near an [[Ancient Pseudoscience Station]]. The Thulecite Crown is head [[Armor]] that will absorb 90% of physical damage and has 1200 durability. When the wearer takes damage from an attack, it has a 33% chance to create a force field. The force field lasts for 4 seconds, preventing all damage from attacks (except for [[Fire]]) and granting stun immunity. While the force field is active, 5% of incoming damage is reduced from [[Sanity]]. The force field cannot occur more than once every 9 seconds. Unequipping the Crown will disable an active force field, even though the animation remains. The Crown's force field emits enough light to protect the player from [[Charlie]]. __NOTOC__ == {{Pic32|Gift Icon}} Downloadable Content == In the ''[[Reign of Giants]]'' DLC, players can gain more thulecite with the use of [[Construction Amulet]]s and [[Deconstruction Staff]]s. In the ''[[Shipwrecked]]'' DLC, Thulecite Crowns can be obtained as a possible reward from the [[Slot Machine]] and [[X Marks the Spot]] treasure [[Chest]]s. It also can always be found in a [[Chest of the Depths]] left behind by the [[Quacken]] after its death, making Thulecite Crowns renewable also in ''Shipwrecked''. == {{Pic32|Prototype}} Tips == * As it is clear from its perk, the Thulecite Crown is more suited for [[Glossary#T|tanking]] enemies instead of [[Glossary#K|kiting]] them. * The Crown is really useful when facing big groups of Mobs like [[Spider]]s, [[Hounds]], and [[Birchnutter]]s, as the force field will protect the players from being stunlocked. * The force field will protect from the special effects of enemy attacks, such as those of [[Frogs]]. * The Crown is a great armor for [[Willow]], [[Maxwell]], and [[Wigfrid]] ({{Pic24|Reign of Giants icon}} only) since these characters can regain their sanity easily. ** On the other hand, the Crown is not efficient when used on [[Woodlegs]] {{Pic24|Shipwrecked icon}}, since his maximum sanity value is lower than other characters and drops constantly when on land. * It is generally advised to also wear disposable body armor to increase the life of the Thulecite Crown. In ''Don't Starve Together'', the [[Log Suit]] can absorb roughly 47% of already absorbed damage, effectively increasing the life of the Thulecite Crown from 840 to 1585. * In the ''[[Don't Starve: Hamlet|Hamlet]]'' DLC, since the Thulecite Crown only triggers its force field when the wearer takes damage, the [[Vortex Cloak]] will prevent it from activating when both are worn simultaneously and the Thulecite Crown is the [[Armor#Calculations|primary armor]].{{Verify|May have been affected by changes from the new Quality of Life update}} If the Vortex Cloak is the primary armor, the Thulecite Crown will not lose durability but also will not trigger the force field. If the Vortex Cloak is at 0% durability, the Thulecite Crown will activate its force field. As such, using both pieces of equipment in combat at the same time is ill-advised in favor of something more disposable such as a [[Football Helmet]]. == {{Pic32|Placeholder}} Trivia == * The Thulecite Crown was added in ''[[Version History#September 10.2C 2013 - A Moderately Friendly Update|A Moderately Friendly Update]]''. * Although the crown showcases a [[Red Gem]], one does not need it to craft the crown. == {{Pic32|Blueprint}} Gallery == Thulecite_Crown_Equipped.png|Thulecite Crown Equipped Thulecite Crown force field.jpg|The Thulecite Crown's force field.
